Never use: HealthStartz Plum (#3A1A4A) · Royal Violet (#7B3FA0) · Vivid Magenta (#C84B8A) in EpiGenIQ materials. Electric Violet (#A855F7) is EpiGenIQ's own accent — brighter, distinct from the parent brand.

08 — Logo Usage Rules

Do & Never Do

✓ Always Do
  • Use only approved JPG/SVG source files — never recreate the wordmark
  • Use long tagline on horizontal lockups — short tagline on square icons
  • Maintain clear space equal to icon mark height on all sides
  • Primary Dark on dark backgrounds · Reverse White on light backgrounds
  • Charge Accent for biology CTAs · Violet Accent for AI and score content
  • Mono Black/White for single-colour print, embossing, engraving
  • Minimum size: 120px wide digital · 1.25 inches wide print
  • #NSBrandAffiliate on all commercial social posts
  • Include ™ on first mention in every document
  • Sit LEFT on video calls — logo background anchored right
✕ Never Do
  • Never stretch, skew, rotate, or distort the logo
  • Never apply drop shadows, glows, or gradients to the logo
  • Never place on busy photography without a colour overlay
  • Never retype or recreate the wordmark in any other font
  • Never use HealthStartz Plum (#3A1A4A · #7B3FA0 · #C84B8A)
  • Never use Launch Maven red (#8E2C2C) in EpiGenIQ materials
  • Never state specific income figures in public posts — ever
  • Never call Prysm iO a "wearable" or "medical device"
  • Never use Inter, Roboto, or Arial as primary typeface
  • Never combine EpiGenIQ + HealthStartz logos without authorisation
Required website disclaimer: "Independent Brand Affiliate Produced Website — Not produced or approved by Nu Skin Enterprises, Inc. or its affiliated companies."
09 — Brand Voice · From EpiGenIQ Field Notes

How EpiGenIQ Speaks

Speaks like a senior research analyst — confident, evidence-led, dryly witty, never breathless. Reference: Stratechery · Not Boring · Peter Attia · The Information.

Direct — not hedging
"The supplement industry is flying blind. Here is what changes that."
Lead with the insight. No "we think." Clients pay for clarity.
Provocative — not preachy
"$200B spent. 40% can't tell if it works. That's a measurement problem."
Take a stance. Thought leadership requires an actual thought.
Precise — not vague
"30 million validated scans. Nobel science. One score."
Specific numbers. Named researchers. Wit by precision, not punchline.
Editorial — not transactional
"Field Notes. 600 words. One insight. Every Friday."
EpiGenIQ educates first. No product pitches in public editorial.
We Do
  • ✓ Lead with insight, then prove it
  • ✓ Cite peer-reviewed work and named researchers
  • ✓ Use specific numbers and data
  • ✓ One strong opinion per issue or post
  • ✓ #NSBrandAffiliate on all commercial content
  • ✓ Structure/function language only for Pharmanex
We Don't
  • ✕ "Revolutionary" "game-changing" "breakthrough"
  • ✕ Disease prevention/treatment/cure language
  • ✕ Direct product pitches in Field Notes editorial
  • ✕ Income claims in public posts
  • ✕ Calling Prysm iO a "wearable" or "medical device"
  • ✕ Testimonials as primary evidence
10 — Spacing System

8px Grid — Everything Aligns

Every spacing decision maps to this scale. Consistent across web, email, print, and presentations.

The Scale
8px
Micro — icon padding, tight gaps
16px
Small — label-to-title, list items
24px
Base — card padding, content gaps
32px
Medium — section header margin
40px
Logo clear space minimum
48px
Large — email section padding
56px
Section padding — page sections
80px
Hero — major section breaks
Type Scale
48–72px — Hero title · Syne 800
28–36px — Page/slide title · DM Serif Display
18–24px — Section heading · Syne 700
14–16px — Subheading · DM Sans 600
15–17px — Body copy · DM Sans 300 · 1.65 leading
9–11px — Eyebrow/label · Syne 700 · ALL CAPS
10–12px — Caption / legal · DM Sans 300
